questions.text: Which biomolecule is the main source of quick energy for cells?
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are the primary source of energy in many organisms.
Proteins are built from amino acids linked by peptide bonds.

questions.text: Which of the following is a lipid?
questions.explanation: Cholesterol is a lipid found in cell membranes.

questions.text: Enzymes are proteins that act as:
questions.explanation: Enzymes speed up biochemical reactions without being consumed.

questions.text: Which nucleic acid carries genetic information?
questions.explanation: DNA stores genetic information, while RNA helps transmit it.

questions.text: The building blocks of nucleic acids are:
questions.explanation: Nucleotides consist of a sugar, phosphate, and nitrogenous base.
